question_title: What will be the value of "Overnight Repurchase Agreements: Treasury Securities Purchased by the Federal Reserve in the Temporary Open Market Operations" on 2026-02-10?
summary: Feb 10, 2026 is the second Tuesday of February, and RPONTSYD shows a remarkably consistent ~$0.1B value on second Tuesdays (9/12 months). The remaining 3/12 showed near-zero. Jan 2026 confirmed the pattern continues (Jan 13 = 0.102). Distribution is extremely concentrated around 0.1 with a thin right tail from potential funding stress. Oct-Dec 2025 stress period has clearly ended based on quiet Jan 2026 data.

Most recent second Tuesday (Jan 13, 2026) = 0.102, confirming the pattern continues
Jan 2026 data shows normal, quiet conditions — no sign of funding stress

## Summary

This question asks about the FRED series RPONTSYD — the daily value of overnight repo operations where the Fed purchases Treasury securities. 

**Key finding:** Feb 10, 2026 is the **second Tuesday of February**, and the data reveals an extremely consistent pattern: the second Tuesday of each month almost always has a value of ~$0.1 billion (100 million). This held for 9 of 12 months in my sample, with the remaining 3 showing near-zero values. The most recent second Tuesday (Jan 13, 2026) confirmed the pattern at 0.102.

The distribution is extremely non-Gaussian — essentially a mixture of:
- **~15%** near-zero (the operation doesn't happen)
- **~75%** at ~0.1B (the regular second-Tuesday operation)
- **~10%** in a wide right tail (unexpected funding stress)

By January 2026, repo operations had returned to normal after the Oct-Dec 2025 stress period (which saw values up to 31.5B). Current conditions appear calm.
